message.content:
Review the uncommitted diff in /Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify against the US-036 story acceptance criteria and Marketify's tech-architecture spec.

## Context

US-036 is "Pull-to-refresh metrics on creator profile (6h throttle)". The UI-layer drag gesture was already committed in `be06466` — the current uncommitted diff is the backend JWT claim restructure that was required to make the authed `metrics-refresh` call succeed end-to-end. Previously every authed PostgREST call was returning `401 PGRST301 "Role claim invalid"` because our JWT had `role: "creator"|"lister"` — but PostgREST treats `role` as the Postgres DB role to switch to, not the app-level role.

## Changes to review

1. `supabase/functions/_shared/jwt.ts` — SignInput now takes `app_role` (creator|lister). signJwt puts `role: "authenticated"` and `app_role: <creator|lister>` in the token payload. verifyJwt now checks `role === "authenticated"` AND `app_role in (creator,lister)`. Still HS256 via `MARKETIFY_JWT_SECRET`.

2. `supabase/migrations/20260418020000_us_jwt_app_role_claim.sql` — replaces `public.current_user_role()` to read from `auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role'` (was `->> 'role'`). This is already applied to the DB project ([REDACTED_SUPABASE_PROJECT_REF]) via Supabase MCP.

3. All 7 edge functions (apply-to-listing, auth-signup-creator, auth-signup-lister, get-listing-detail, manage-social-link, metrics-refresh, submit-video): the gate checks changed from `claims.role !== "creator"` to `claims.app_role !== "creator"`, and signJwt calls now pass `app_role: ...` instead of `role: ...`. All 7 edge functions are already deployed to the project.

4. `app/(auth)/_layout.tsx` — Redirect paths corrected to match actual route files (`/(creator)/feed`, `/(lister)/dashboard`).

5. `scripts/test/probe-us036-refresh.ts` — NEW probe script. Signs up a fresh creator → calls PostgREST social_links with the JWT → calls metrics-refresh with the social_link_id. Verifies the full backend path. Returned 200 on last run with `{status: "queued", metrics_status: {tiktok: "fresh"}}`.

## Relevant spec sections

- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.4 (auth, JWT shape, RLS helpers) — this is what the new shape is supposed to align with.
- `docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.7 (DB schema — `user_role` enum is the app-level type, `public.current_user_role()` the RLS helper).

## What I want

Focus on:
- Correctness of the JWT claim restructure — any RLS function or edge-function gate that still reads the old `role` claim?
- Consistency: all 7 edge functions caller sites + callee side.
- Security: any edge function that previously dispatched based on `claims.role` but was missed in the rename?
- Any stale tooling/test fixtures that will break because they still use the old shape (e.g. scripts under `scripts/test/` — I noticed `scripts/test/metrics-refresh.ts` still references `role: "creator"|"lister"` and will fail, flag that).

Report any medium-or-higher issues. Skip minor style nits. Under 400 words.

todos.content: Read AGENTS.md and tech-architecture spec sections
Read the diff of all changed files
Check for stale role references across edge functions and test scripts

content.content: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

1	# Marketify — Agent Conventions
2	
3	These rules apply to **every** AI coding agent (Claude Code, Amp, Codex, etc.) that touches this repo. They are read on every fresh context.
4	
5	## Authoritative specs
6	
7	- `docs/product-plan.md` — canonical product, scope, build sequence
8	- `docs/design.md` — visual language, components, screens, motion
9	- `docs/tech-architecture.md` — DB schema, RLS, triggers, Apify integration, edge-function specs
10	
11	If a task is ambiguous, the specs decide. Do not invent product behavior.
12	
13	## Stack
14	
15	- **Mobile:** Expo (managed) + React Native + TypeScript (strict)
16	- **Backend:** Supabase (Postgres + Edge Functions + Realtime)
17	- **Auth:** custom HS256 JWT signed by our own edge function (NOT Supabase Auth) — see `docs/product-plan.md §2`
18	- **Metrics:** Apify scrapers (no OAuth) — see `docs/tech-architecture.md §3`
19	- **Package manager:** `bun` (do not use npm/yarn/pnpm)
20	- **Animations:** Reanimated 3 worklets
21	- **Icons:** Lucide React Native (no emoji in product UI)
22	- **Routing:** expo-router
23	- **State:** React context + MMKV for persisted slices
24	
25	## MCP usage (required, not optional)
26	
27	- **Supabase MCP (`mcp__plugin_supabase_supabase__*`)** for ALL DB operations: migrations (`apply_migration`), queries (`execute_sql`), edge function deploys (`deploy_edge_function`), type generation (`generate_typescript_types`), security advisors (`get_advisors`). Do **not** use the local `supabase` CLI for these.
28	- **mobile-mcp (`mcp__mobile-mcp__*`)** for any UI verification on iOS simulator. A UI story is not done without a screenshot from `mobile_take_screenshot`.
29	- **Context7 MCP (`mcp__plugin_context7_context7__*`)** for library docs (Expo, Reanimated, Supabase JS, expo-router) — your training data may be stale.
30	- **Apify** is called from edge functions, not the app. Key is `APIFY_KEY` in `.env`.
31	
32	## Quality gates (every commit)
33	
34	1. `bun run typecheck` (`tsc --noEmit`) — zero errors
35	2. `bun run lint` (`expo lint`) — no new warnings
36	3. After any schema/RLS change: `get_advisors({type: "security"})` — zero error-level findings
37	4. For UI changes: mobile-mcp verification + screenshot saved under `scripts/ralph/screenshots/`
38	5. Code review by `feature-dev:code-reviewer` subagent before commit
39	
40	## Migration discipline
41	
42	- Append-only — never edit a previously applied migration
43	- Named `us_NNN_short_description` (matches the Ralph story ID when applicable)
44	- Always regenerate `src/types/supabase.ts` and commit it in the same commit
45	- Always run security advisors after RLS / schema changes
46	
47	## Folder layout
48	
49	- `app/` — expo-router routes (grouped by `(auth)`, `(creator)`, `(lister)`)
50	- `src/components/primitives/` — reusable atoms (Button, Chip, etc.)
51	- `src/components/shared/` — cross-feature composites (EmptyState, ErrorState)
52	- `src/screens/` — screen-specific composites
53	- `src/lib/` — supabase client, auth context, utilities
54	- `src/design/` — tokens, typography, motion
55	- `src/types/` — including generated `supabase.ts`
56	- `supabase/functions/<name>/` — edge functions
57	- `supabase/functions/_shared/` — shared edge-function utilities
58	- `supabase/migrations/` — append-only SQL
59	- `scripts/ralph/` — autonomous loop config (do not modify mid-loop)
60	- `docs/` — authoritative specs (do not edit unless story explicitly requires)
61	
62	## Hard scope rules (v1)
63	
64	- No payments — `price_cents` is display-only
65	- No platform OAuth — Apify only
66	- One role per account — no dual-role
67	- No push notifications — in-app only
68	- No revision-request — binary approve/reject
69	- No dark mode
70

content.content:
439-Custom JWT issued by our `/auth/*` edge functions (signed with project JWT secret). Payload:
440-```json
441-{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
442-```
443:RLS policies read `auth.jwt() ->> 'sub'`. JWT is signed by us, not user-editable. Safe for authz.
444-
445-### 4.5 Indexes
446-
447-- Feed: partial index `(status, created_at DESC) WHERE status='active'`.
--
838-alter table public.events enable row level security;
839-
840-create or replace function public.current_user_id() returns uuid
841-language sql stable as $$
842:  select nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'sub', '')::uuid
843-$$;
844:create or replace function public.current_user_role() returns user_role
845-language sql stable as $$
846:  select (auth.jwt() ->> 'role')::user_role
847-$$;
848-
849--- Design principles:
850---   1. Every policy filters by `current_user_id()` — JWT-derived, never query param.
--

content.content: Todos have been modified successfully. Ensure that you continue to use the todo list to track your progress. Please proceed with the current tasks if applicable

1	-- The Marketify JWT now puts the Postgres role under the standard `role` claim
2	-- (always "authenticated") and the app-level role under `app_role`. PostgREST
3	-- uses `role` to switch DB role; RLS reads the app role through this helper.
4	create or replace function public.current_user_role()
5	returns public.user_role
6	language sql
7	stable
8	set search_path = ''
9	as $$
10	  select nullif(auth.jwt() ->> 'app_role', '')::public.user_role
11	$$;

/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/ralph/progress.txt-36-- **`listing_conditions` is a single data-driven table**, not separate pre/post tables. Discriminated by `condition_kind` enum ('pre' | 'post') per docs/tech-architecture.md §4.7 block 8. Pre-condition rows use metrics like `min_followers`; post-condition rows use `post_*` metrics. The story US-005 text named separate tables but the spec wins.
scripts/ralph/progress.txt-37-- **Circular FK pattern:** `listings.current_version_id` references `listing_versions(id)` but both tables are in the same migration. Create the `listings` table with the column nullable, create `listing_versions`, then `alter table public.listings add constraint ... foreign key ...`. Spec follows this pattern (§4.7 lines 659-661).
/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/ralph/progress.txt-38-- **Supabase defaults views to `security_invoker=off` (effectively DEFINER)**; it will fail the `security_definer_view` advisor. Every view we create must be followed with `alter view <name> set (security_invoker = on)` to match the project standard at §4.1 line 375. Applied to `submission_reuse_view` in US-006.

content.text:
Reviewing: git diff (unstaged) for the US-036 JWT claim restructure across `_shared/jwt.ts`, 7 edge functions, the migration, `app/(auth)/_layout.tsx`, and the test scripts.

---

## Critical

**Stale `signMarketifyJwt` in 4 test scripts — mints old-shape tokens, all assertions will fail** (Confidence: 100)

The `signMarketifyJwt` helper is copy-pasted into four test scripts that were NOT updated as part of this diff:

- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/test/metrics-refresh.ts` — lines 63-64, 239, 257, 274-276
- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/test/apply-to-listing.ts` — lines 70-71, 232, 251, 270
- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/test/get-listing-detail.ts` — lines 59-60, 191, 208, 222
- `/Users/bulutyazgan/Desktop/marketify/scripts/test/submit-video.ts` — lines 75-76, 286, 312, 335

Each helper has the signature:
```
claims: { sub: string; role: "creator" | "lister"; session_id: string }
```
and builds a payload with `role: "creator"` or `role: "lister"`. After this restructure, `verifyJwt` in `_shared/jwt.ts` checks `payload.role !== "authenticated"` and throws `"JWT missing required claims"`. Every test assertion that relies on a creator or lister JWT signed by this local helper will now return 401, not the expected status. This affects the majority of the test matrix in all four scripts (assertions 3-11 in `metrics-refresh.ts`, for example). You called out `metrics-refresh.ts` specifically — confirmed, and the same problem exists identically in the other three.

Fix: in each script, update `signMarketifyJwt` to accept `app_role` instead of `role`, set `role: "authenticated"` and `app_role` in the payload, and update all call sites accordingly. Pattern from the correct shape in `_shared/jwt.ts` lines 94-101.

---

## Important

**`docs/tech-architecture.md` §4.4 and the `current_user_role()` snippet at line 846 still document the old JWT shape** (Confidence: 85)

The spec at line 441-442 reads:
```json
{ "sub": "<user_id>", "role": "creator|lister", "session_id": "<uuid>" }
```
And the helper at line 846 reads `auth.jwt() ->> 'role'`. The live DB function has been replaced by the new migration but the spec is now contradictory — any agent reading it as authoritative will regress this change. AGENTS.md states "docs/ — authoritative specs (do not edit unless story explicitly requires)". Updating the spec requires an explicit story or a note in `progress.txt` flagging the drift; otherwise the next Ralph iteration that reads §4.4 will regenerate JWT code with `role: "creator"` instead of `app_role`.

---

## No Issues Found

The 7 edge functions themselves are all consistent: every `verifyJwt` call path, every `claims.app_role` gate check, and every `signJwt({ app_role: ... })` mint in the auth-signup functions are correct. The new migration properly uses `CREATE OR REPLACE` to supersede the old `us_009_rls_policies.sql` definition. The `app/(auth)/_layout.tsx` redirect paths are a simple correctness fix with no interaction with the JWT change.
